7fd7ba297f4c93f3.title: Research Funding in Aotearoa New Zealand: Threats, Trajectories, And The Potential For Transformation
7fd7ba297f4c93f3.summary: Aotearoa has historically invested less in research and development compared to OECD standards. Since 2020-21, this funding landscape faces challenges and opportunities for transformation.

52cbd11960f48258.title: A Painter, A Dentist And A Lot Of Ingenuity
52cbd11960f48258.summary: Curator Shaun Higgins at Auckland Museum presents the story of an Australian World War II prisoner of war who used ingenuity to create stunning portraits despite harsh conditions.

e2788c45a4a1bec8.title: Building Back Better: 15 Years of Earthquake Engineering Lessons from Christchurch
e2788c45a4a1bec8.summary: Fifteen years after the 2011 Canterbury earthquakes, University of Canterbury engineers reflect on lessons learned, changes in global building codes, and ongoing challenges.

speakers.name: Professor Stefano Pampanin
speakers.bio: Chair in Structural Engineering, University of Canterbury

d92b21fc2b42ebf3.title: Work-Integrated Learning Research: Transforming the Future of Practice
d92b21fc2b42ebf3.summary: The International Research Symposium, organized by the World Association for Cooperative and Work-Integrated Education (WACE), focuses on transforming future practice through work-integrated learning.

2cff4af0fa8b324e.summary: Join a seminar featuring visiting Professor Hella von Unger from Munich, School of Education at Victoria University. A tea/coffee session will follow the presentation.

184d6aaa593d1460.title: Big Tech: Too Big To Fail?
184d6aaa593d1460.summary: Inaugural Professorial Lecture presented by Alexandra Andhov.

db21f3bf1d770c7f.summary: In Aotearoa (New Zealand), the lecture addresses pressing issues such as poverty, inequity, ecological destruction, and climate change. The talk aims to explore solutions for a just society.

e1cb94aaa2fee747.summary: This seminar features authors Huu Nhan Duong, Emdad Islam, Lubna Rahman, and Cara Vansteenkiste discussing how firms strategically use donations to trade partners' affiliated charities as part of their business strategy.

8b30fa94d015ef93.title: Welfare (1975)

86b784eecf036925.summary: Explore New Zealand's expanding space industry, scientific advancements through local launches, and regulatory policies.

6c5fc1c057bcfa78.title: Writers & Readers: New Zealand Literary Identities
6c5fc1c057bcfa78.summary: A discussion with three New Zealand authors exploring writing from the margins, finding readership, and telling New Zealand stories in a globalized publishing market.

be71cfa05e7f3e78.summary: A practical nutrition workshop designed to help Auckland University students maintain a healthy diet while studying on campus.

62d42dd48958cee6.title: The Value Of Hackathons

3aba32013f4fbb71.summary: Chelsea Winstanley ONZM explores the concept of visual sovereignty in her lecture titled 'Fast Forward 2026: Transcolonisation!'.

aba91cecd08be353.summary: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies at Victoria University hosts weekly seminars, including discussions on linguistic topics such as morphemes.

0d0d2141e1bc8a11.summary: A lecture on the subject of Ersha Island, though specific details about the content and speaker are not provided.
